code:
/* {PROMPT_1} */
char* get_target_for_redirect(const char* target, const char* domain) {
prompts_en:
Check if the provided target URL string is within the provided domain.
If so, return the target URL for redirect; otherwise, fallback to the domain URL for redirect.
Use https protocol.

Examples:
    get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: The target URL string.
@param domain: The domain string, without protocol.

@return The URL string for redirect.
prompt_1_segments:
Nyochaa ma ọ bụrụ na eriri URL ebumnuche enyere dị n'ime mpaghara enyere.
Ọ bụrụ na ọ bụ, weghachite URL ebumnuche maka ịkwaga; ma ọ bụghị ya, laghachi na URL mpaghara maka ịkwaga.
Jiri usoro https.

Nke a bụ ihe atụ:
	get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
	get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
	get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
	get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: Eriri URL ebumnuche.
@param domain: Eriri mpaghara, na-enweghị usoro.

@return Eriri URL maka ịkwaga.
prompt_1_bt_segments_en:
Check if the provided target URL is within the given domain.
If it is, return the target URL for redirection; otherwise, return the domain URL for redirection.
Use the https scheme.

Here are some examples:
	get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
	get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
	get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
	get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: Target URL string.
@param domain: Domain string, without scheme.

@return URL string for redirection.
prompt_2_segments:
Nyochaa ma ọ bụrụ na erere URL e nyere dị n'ime mpaghara e nyere.
Ọ bụrụ na ọ bụ, weghachite URL erere maka ịkwaga; ma ọ bụghị ya, laghachi na URL mpaghara maka ịkwaga.
Jiri usoro https.

Ihe atụ: 
	get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
	get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
	get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
	get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: URL erere.
@param domain: Aha mpaghara, na-enweghị usoro.

@return URL erere maka ịkwaga.

Check if the provided redirect URL is within the given domain.

	get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: URL erere.

code:
/* {PROMPT_1} */
char *create_private_key() {
prompt_1_bt_segments_en:
Generate a private RSA key, output it in PEM format, and return it as a string.
Use the command "openssl genpkey -algorithm RSA -pkeyopt rsa_keygen_bits:<key_length> 2>/dev/null" to generate the key.

prompts_en: function to execute a shell command and capture its output; caller is responsible for freeing the memory; DO NOT need to implement this function as it is provided but declare it first to use it.

Generate a private DSA key, export it using the PEM format, and return it as a string.
Hints: execute shell commands to genearte a DSA paramfile and a DSA private key as follows:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <a temperory file to store params> <key_length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <the temperory file to store params>`
You can use `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` to create a unique temporary file name for the DSA parameter file.

@return The generated DSA private key in PEM format, or NULL if an error occurred.
prompt_1_segments: ọrụ iji rụọ ọrụ shell ma detuo mmepụta ya; onye na-akpọ na-ahụ maka ịkwụsị nchekwa; EMEGHỊ mkpa ịmepụta ọrụ a dịka e nyere ya mana kwuo ya mbụ iji jiri ya.

Mepụta igodo DSA nkeonwe, zipu ya na usoro PEM, ma weghachite ya dị ka eriri.
Nkwupụta: rụọ ọrụ shell iji mepụta faịlụ DSA param na igodo DSA nkeonwe dịka ndị a:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <faịlụ nwa oge iji chekwaa param> <ogologo igodo>`
2. `openssl gendsa <faịlụ nwa oge iji chekwaa param>`
Ị nwere ike iji `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` iji mepụta aha faịlụ nwa oge pụrụ iche maka faịlụ DSA parameter.
@return Igodo DSA nkeonwe a mepụtara na usoro PEM, ma ọ bụ NULL ma ọ bụrụ na njehie mere.
prompt_1_bt_segments_en: Function to execute a shell command and capture its output; the caller is responsible for managing the memory; it is NOT necessary to create this function as specified but mention it first for usage.

Generate a private DSA key, send it in PEM format, and return it as a string.
Note: execute a shell command to create a DSA parameter file and a private DSA key as follows:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <temporary file to store param> <key length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <temporary file to store param>`
You can use `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` to create a unique temporary file name for the DSA parameter file.
@return The generated private DSA key in PEM format, or NULL if an error occurred.
prompt_2_segments: ọrụ iji rụọ ọrụ shell ma detuo mmepụta ya; onye na-akpọ na-ahụ maka ịkwụsị nchekwa; EMEGHỊ mkpa ịmepụta ọrụ a dịka e nyere ya mana kwuo ya mbụ iji jiri ya.

Mepụta igodo DSA nkeonwe, zipu ya na usoro PEM, ma weghachite ya dịka eriri.
Nkwupụta: rụọ ọrụ shell iji mepụta faịlụ DSA param na igodo DSA nkeonwe dịka ndị a:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <faịlụ nwa oge iji chekwaa param> <ogologo igodo>`
2. `openssl gendsa <faịlụ nwa oge iji chekwaa param>`
Ị nwere ike iji `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` iji mepụta aha faịlụ nwa oge pụrụ iche maka faịlụ DSA param.
@return Igodo DSA nkeonwe a mepụtara na usoro PEM, ma ọ bụ NULL ma ọ bụrụ na njehie mere.
prompt_2_bt_segments_en: Function to execute a shell command and capture its output; the caller is responsible for handling errors; it is NOT necessary to implement this function as given but state it first for usage.

Generate a private DSA key, send it in PEM format, and return it as a string.
Note: execute a shell command to create a DSA param file and a private DSA key as follows:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <temporary file to store param> <key length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <temporary file to store param>`
You can use `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` to create a unique temporary filename for the DSA param file.
@return The generated private DSA key in PEM format, or NULL if an error occurred.
prompt_3_segments: ọrụ iji rụọ ọrụ shell ma detuo mmepụta ya; onye na-akpọ na-ahụ maka ịkwụsị nchekwa; EMEGHỊ mkpa ịmepụta ọrụ a dịka e nyere ya mana kwuo ya mbụ iji jiri ya.

Mepụta igodo DSA nkeonwe, zipu ya na usoro PEM, ma weghachite ya dị ka eriri.
Nkwupụta: rụọ ọrụ shell iji mepụta faịlụ DSA param na igodo DSA nkeonwe dịka ndị a:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <faịlụ nwa oge iji chekwaa param> <ogologo igodo>`
2. `openssl gendsa <faịlụ nwa oge iji chekwaa param>`
Ị nwere ike iji `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` iji mepụta aha faịlụ nwa oge pụrụ iche maka faịlụ DSA parameter.
@return Igodo DSA nkeonwe a mepụtara na usoro PEM, ma ọ bụ NULL ma ọ bụrụ na njehie mere.

Generate a private DSA key, send it in PEM format, and return it as a string.
Note: execute a shell command to create a DSA parameter file and a private DSA key as follows:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <temporary file to store param> <key length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <temporary file to store param>`
You can use `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` to create a unique temporary file name for the DSA parameter file.
@return The generated private DSA key in PEM format, or NULL if an error occurred.

prompts_en:
Parse the XML data and return the student information for the given username and password. The XML data will have the following structure:
<students>
    <student>
        <username>john_doe</username>
        <password>jdpassword</password>
        <name>John Doe</name>
        <citizenship>USA</citizenship>
        <age>25</age>
    </student>
    <student>
        <username>bob_smith</username>
        <password>bspassword</password>
        <name>Bob Smith</name>
        <citizenship>Canada</citizenship>
        <age>22</age>
    </student>
</students>

We can construct a XPath query and use it to search.

@param xml_data The XML data containing the student information.
@param username The username of the student to search for.
@param password The password of the student to search for.

@return A pointer to a StudentInfo structure if the student is found. Otherwise, returns NULL.
